events.text: In general, administering insulin without knowing blood glucose levels[br4.388][br4.469]and double-checking the EHR can lead to medication errors.[br8.448][br8.529]Even after carefully checking the EHR, it's important to consider all possible information and cues,[br15.154][br15.235]like blood glucose level, because there could be a system error if Rhea's postop orders were not updated properly.[br22.843][br22.924]In this case, Vic followed what he saw in the EHR and administered the 3 units of insulin.[br29.113][br29.194]This is an example of an individual error.[br31.928]

When I checked on Rhea just a few minutes later, I found her confused and agitated.[br5.681][br5.762]She was sweating profusely and seemed lethargic. What do these cues suggest was most likely happening with Rhea?[br13.048]
